Task  
MODULE 1: FRAMEING THE TASK  
THIS MODULE DEFINES THE SCOPE AND BOUNDARIES OF THE TASK. IT OUTLINES THE MAIN COMPONENTS OF THE SYSTEM PROMPT AND CLARIFIES THE OBJECTIVES AND CONSTRAINTS THAT MUST BE FOLLOWED. THE TASK IS TO ASERT IF MOVIE (1) AND MOVIE (2) ARE REFERRING TO THE SAME ENTITY OR NOT. THE FINAL RESPONSE MUST BE "YES" OR "NO". THE SYSTEM MUST BE DESIGN TO HANDLE POTENTIAL ERRORS AND VALIDATE THE OUTPUT AGAINST THE ORIGINAL PROMPT'S REQUIREMENTS.  

MODULE 2: REASONING PROCESS  
THIS MODULE FOCUSES ON THE LOGICAL STEPS AND REASONING MECHANISMS THAT WILL BE USED TO SOLVE THE TASK. IT INCLUDES THE METHODS AND ALGORITHMS THAT WILL BE APPLIED TO ACHIEVE THE DESIRED OUTCOME. THE SYSTEM WILL ANALYZE THE DATA IN THE TWO TABLES TO IDENTIFY IF THE MOVIES ARE REFERRING TO THE SAME ENTITY. THIS INCLUDES COMPARING THE YEAR, DIRECTOR, TITLE, WRITERS, AND ACTORS OF THE TWO MOVIES. THE SYSTEM WILL CHECK FOR ANY DISAGREEMENTS IN THESE FIELDS AND DETERMINE IF THE MOVIES ARE THE SAME OR NOT.  

MODULE 3: FORMATTING AND OUTPUT  
THIS MODULE SPECIFIES THE FORMAT IN WHICH THE FINAL OUTPUT MUST BE PRESENTED. IT INCLUDES DETAILS ABOUT THE STRUCTURE, CONTENT, AND STYLE OF THE RESPONSE. THE FINAL OUTPUT MUST BE IN THE FORMAT {"answer": "<Yes or No>"} AND MUST BE IN ENGLISH. THE WORD "YES" OR "NO" MUST BE IN ALL CAPS. THE SYSTEM MUST GENERATE A RESPONSE THAT IS COMPLETE, ACCURATE, AND MEETS ALL SPECIFIED REQUIREMENTS.  

MODULE 4: ERROR PREVENTION AND VALIDATION  
THIS MODULE ENDS THE SYSTEM'S LOGIC TO HANDLE POTENTIAL ERRORS AND VALIDATE THE OUTPUT AGAINST THE ORIGINAL PROMPT'S REQUIREMENTS. THE SYSTEM WILL CHECK FOR ANY ERRORS IN THE DATA INPUT, SUCH AS INCORRECT YEARS, DIRECTORS, OR ACTORS. IT WILL VALIDATE THAT THE MOVIES ARE REFERRING TO THE SAME ENTITY BASED ON THE DATA PROVIDED. THE SYSTEM WILL OUTPUT "YES" OR "NO" BASED ON THE ANALYSIS AND WILL PREVENT ANY INVALID OR INACCURATE RESPONSES.  

{"answer": "YES
